summary: Excellent matrix recording with crystal‑clear sound captures a stellar 1991 show featuring a soaring Dark Star/Other One pair, powerful Eyes of the World and an unforgettable Brokedown Palace encore.
review: Both reviewers rave about the ultramatrix remaster – the audio is crisp, balanced and free of noticeable flaws. The concert itself is described as a first‑set “strong” opener followed by a second set that builds to a magical Dark Star → Saint → The Other One sequence, capped off by a rare Brokedown Palace/We Bid You Goodnight encore. Jerry’s guitar work, Phil’s bass interplay and the tasteful keyboard contributions round out an unforgettable performance.

ai_rating.rationale: Two detailed 5‑star reviews highlight both superb musical performance and top‑notch ultramatrix sound; the weight of show quality (70%) and recording quality (30%) yields an overall rating near the top of the scale.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Ultramatrix remaster praised as 'top notch' and 'crisp'; no drop‑outs or muddiness mentioned.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set described as strong and well‑played; long break leads into a second set that opens with Dark Star and flows through Saint, Eyes of the World, The Other One and culminates in an emotionally charged encore.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for gorgeous tone, emotive solos (Dark Star, Eyes of the World, Brokedown Palace) and charismatic stage presence.
band_member_comments.Phil: Highlighted for excellent interaction, especially on The Other One and during vocal acknowledgements at the encore.
band_member_comments.Bob: Noted for passionate vocals on Good Lovin', Attics of My Life and the heartfelt delivery on the encore.
band_member_comments.Keys: Bruce’s accordion/keys described as heavy on some songs but balanced with Vince; overall complementary to the band.
band_member_comments.Drums: Described as cerebral, building intensity and supporting the psychedelic flow of the second set.
